message:
fix(shape-stride): zero-sized axes match NumPy's (0, ..., 0) stride rule

Closes a real divergence between fnp and NumPy's stride calculus
engine on any array whose shape contains a zero-sized axis: NumPy
emits all-zero strides (because there is no element to address), but
the previous fnp path was multiplying the later-dimension sizes and
emitting non-zero strides that would never be dereferenced but still
failed contiguity / round-trip conformance checks.

crates/fnp-ndarray/src/lib.rs:

- broadcast_shape now threads the () + (0, 3) -> (0, 3) case through
  the scalar-vs-ndim arm rather than falling through to the generic
  broadcast that would reject a zero extent. New unit test
  broadcast_scalar_with_zero_sized_target locks it in.
- broadcast_strides honours the (), (0, 3) -> (0, 0) contract for
  strides, plus a rejection test for broadcast_strides((1,), ...,
  ()) since broadcast_shapes((1,), ()) is (1,) but broadcast_to into
  () still fails in NumPy. Both cases are now exercised as unit
  tests.

crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs:

- c_strides_elems, f_strides_elems, and contiguous_strides_elems all
  short-circuit to vec![0; ndim] when any axis is zero, so
  UFuncArray::new(shape, [], dtype).strides() returns (0, 0) instead
  of whatever the generic stride multiplier produced. Added
  strides_zero_sized_arrays_match_numpy as a regression test.

crates/fnp-conformance/fixtures/shape_stride_cases.json:

- Adds six new cases exercising empty-first-axis / empty-middle-
  axis / empty-last-axis broadcasts, each pinned to the
  (0, 3, 4) / (2, 0, 4) / (2, 3, 0) shapes with expected_strides
  (0, 0, 0) and reason_code tags so the conformance report
  explains why the zero-axis cases exist. All six carry
  artifact_refs pointing at the FNP-P2C-001 fixture manifest so
  the artifact tracking link stays alive when CI re-emits
  reports.
